title: Citrusy Roasted Ukrainian Cumin Fritters
description: A one-pot Ukrainian dish featuring cumin, agemono-deep-fry, and a citrusy twist, perfect for dairy-free diets, with a delicious cheese-pull.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt.
Add the vegan butter or margarine and, using a pastry blender or your fingertips, work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the grated carrot, chopped fresh dill, and minced garlic.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the non-dairy milk, vegan mozzarella shreds, and a pinch of salt.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat the olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add 2-3 fritters to the skillet (do not overcrowd).
Cook the fritters for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Transfer the fritters to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast in a preheated oven at 40041F (20026C) for 10-12 minutes, or until they are crispy and golden brown.
Serve the citrusy roasted Ukrainian cumin fritters hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice, if desired.
